model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Villanova gets the edge on baseline team quality: the available efficiency ratings have the Wildcats in the low-to-mid 30s nationally with a balanced profile (top-45 offense and top-40 defense), which is typically enough to survive an 8/9 coin-flip. Utah State has a credible upset path if it can win the three-point/tempo variance and keep Villanova out of rhythm, but without a clear ratings case in the data I could access (and no definitive, matchup-flipping availability news found), I’m siding with Villanova’s steadier two-way floor.

reasoningStep.title: West 8/9 decision anchored on efficiency baseline and upset path
reasoningStep.summary: Pick Villanova in a close 8/9 based on the stronger available efficiency profile and a more reliable two-way floor, while acknowledging Utah State’s variance-driven upset route.
evidence: Villanova efficiency baseline: KenPom rank 33 (AdjEM +19.94; AdjO 120.4, #42; AdjD 100.4) and Torvik rank 36 (AdjO ~119.6; AdjD ~100.5).
Utah State ratings were not returned by the ratings lookup tool in this run, limiting direct metric-to-metric comparison.
News scan: web results suggested Villanova wing Matt Hodge suffered a season-ending ACL injury; no specific Utah State availability item surfaced in the tool results.

Matchup framing: in an 8/9 game, the underdog’s most credible path is three-point/turnover variance; absent a clear statistical edge for Utah State in accessible data, Villanova’s balanced two-way profile is the tiebreaker.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
